page: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is a virtual server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, configured with 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz under a shared allocation model. The hardware profile is characterized by a high memory-to-CPU ratio, providing 32.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled as a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core execution profile. The stress-ng single-core CPU benchmark places this instance in the top 10% of tested servers, supported by strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. Memory performance is also robust, with the PassMark memory score and large-block memory write bandwidth ranking in the top 25%. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces performance tradeoffs in multi-threaded scenarios; multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, static web serving throughput, and database operations fall into the bottom 25% of benchmarks.

This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without the premium cost of dedicated CPU cores. The high memory density makes it a practical choice for memory-bound applications, caching layers such as Redis, and single-threaded data processing tasks. It is less suitable for high-throughput database engines or intensive multi-threaded compute workloads due to the shared CPU resource limits.
description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on an x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth. However, its shared CPU allocation leads to lower relative performance in multi-threaded decompression and database operations. This hardware configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications and caching systems like Redis, where memory capacity is prioritized over dedicated multi-core processing power.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is an AMD EPYC virtual server with 4 shared vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe SSD, offering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-heavy, single-core workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense shared-CPU virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive single-threaded applications.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs with 32.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast local data access
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth
Moderate multi-core capabilities with weaker multi-threaded decompression
Best suited for caching layers, Redis, and memory-bound single-threaded tasks
